title: Tech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Tech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Instagram’s TV app is launching on Google TV devices
items.description: By bringing Reels to TVs, Instagram looking to better compete with YouTube, which largely dominates the TV space.

items.title: Ukraine’s startups keep building
items.description: In the four years since Russia’s full-scale invasion of their country, Ukrainian startups have done more than survive: they are still building and gro...

items.title: Meta strikes up to $100B AMD chip deal as it chases ‘personal superintelligence’
items.description: Meta is buying billions of dollars in AMD AI chips in a multiyear deal tied to a 160 million-share warrant, deepening its push to diversify beyond Nvi...

items.title: Discord delays global rollout of age verification after backlash
items.description: The company added that 90% of users won’t need to verify their age and will be able to keep using Discord as usual.

items.title: Oura launches a proprietary AI model focused on women’s health
items.description: The model supports questions spanning the full reproductive health spectrum, from early menstrual cycles through menopause.

items.title: More startups are hitting $10M ARR in 3 months than ever before
items.description: AI has brought the startup world the rise of startups that instantly hit multimillion ARR. Stripe revealed some data that shows how common this has be...

items.title: Anthropic launches new push for enterprise agents with plugins for finance, engineering, and design
items.description: It's a major opportunity to grow Anthropic’s enterprise client base — and a significant threat to SaaS products currently performing those functions.

items.title: Final 4 days to save up to $680 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
items.description: Just 4 days left before savings of up to $680 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass end on February 27 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register to save at one of the...

items.title: Instagram head pressed on lengthy delay to launch teen safety features, like a nudity filter, court filing reveals
items.description: An email chain with Instagram head Adam Mosseri indicated the company was aware of teen safety issues in DMs in 2018, but didn't launch its unwanted n...

items.title: Treasury sanctions Russian zero-day broker accused of buying exploits stolen from U.S. defense contractor
